title: 7 Herbal Creams That Provide Instant Cystitis Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for cystitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the bladder, making it easier to heal and recover from the infection.

Tannins are good for cystitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract, making it easier to pass urine and alleviate symptoms of the condition.
Polyphenols are good for cystitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the bladder and urinary tract.
Isorhapontigenin is good for cystitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and pain in the bladder and urinary tract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, is a plant with many healing properties. The cream made from this plant is often used to treat cystitis, a painful bladder infection. It works by reducing inflammation and relieving pain. The cream contains compounds that help to soothe and calm the bladder and urinary tract. It also has antimicrobial properties, which help to fight off the bacteria causing the infection. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, it can help to reduce discomfort and promote healing. Additionally, the cream is gentle and non-irritating, making it a good choice for people who are sensitive to other medications.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal creams that help with cystitis, start by harvesting fresh Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from a clean and organic source. Rinse the plant parts gently with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, dry the Echinacea flowers and leaves in a warm, dark place, or using a food dehydrator on a low setting. Once dry, crush the plant parts into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder. Combine 2 parts of the powdered Echinacea with 1 part of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil to help preserve the mixture and prevent spoilage. Stir the mixture well and let it sit for a few days to allow the Echinacea to infuse into the oil. After a few days, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, whip the infused oil with a small amount of beeswax to thicken it and create a smooth, spreadable cream. This Echinacea cream can be applied topically to the affected area to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for cystitis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for cystitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease the pain associated with the condition.
Kaempferol is good for cystitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with bladder infections.

Tannins are good for cystitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the bladder lining, making them a natural remedy to alleviate symptoms of the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum cream is sometimes used to help with cystitis. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. The cream may also have antimicrobial properties, which help to fight off infections that can cause cystitis. Cystitis is often caused by a bacterial infection in the bladder, and Hypericum perforatum cream may help to clear this infection. Additionally, the cream may help to soothe the bladder and reduce irritation, making it a potential treatment option for people with cystitis. Some people find that using Hypericum perforatum cream helps to relieve their symptoms and feel more comfortable. The cream is applied directly to the skin, typically around the lower abdomen, and is often used in combination with other treatments, such as antibiotics or pain relief medication.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal creams that help with cystitis, you will first need to harvest or purchase dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, roots, or a combination of both. Start by making a tea by steeping 1-2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then let it cool. Next, combine the cooled tea with 1/4 cup of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and let it steep for another 2-3 hours or overnight in the refrigerator. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. Then, mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the beeswax and shea butter have melted, slowly pour the infused oil mixture into the melted mixture and stir well. Remove from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean container. This Echinacea cream can be applied topically to the affected area to help soothe and reduce inflammation associated with cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for cystitis

Phenolic acids are good for cystitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract, and also prevent bacterial growth that can cause infections.
Silicic acid is good for cystitis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the urinary tract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail, has been used for centuries to help with bladder problems. The cream made from its roots and stems contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. This makes it effective in soothing the bladder and urinary tract, reducing swelling and pain. The cream helps to calm the bladder lining, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by cystitis. It also helps to fight off infections that can cause cystitis, making it easier to recover. Some people find that applying Equisetum arvense cream directly to the affected area helps to ease discomfort and speed up healing. The natural ingredients in the cream are gentle on the skin and can be used alongside other treatments, making it a popular choice for people looking for a natural remedy for cystitis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Echinacea purpurea herbal cream that helps with cystitis, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves. Clean and dry the herbs thoroughly to remove any dirt or moisture. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves with 4 cups of boiling water in a large bowl, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes to create a strong herbal tea. After straining the tea, combine 1 cup of the cooled tea with 1/2 cup of coconut oil or other carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir occasionally until the mixture reaches 160°F to 180°F (71°C to 82°C), then remove from heat and let it cool. Once cooled, add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil, and stir until the mixture thickens and becomes a smooth cream. Pour the cream into small containers, and store them in the refrigerator to keep the cream fresh for up to 6 months. When using the cream, gently apply it to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm the symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal creams for cystitis

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata cream is a natural remedy that can help soothe and heal cystitis. Cystitis is an inflammation of the bladder, often caused by a bacterial infection. The cream contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. It also has antimicrobial properties, helping to fight off the bacteria causing the infection. This can help the body heal faster and prevent future infections. Plantago lanceolata cream can also help to soothe the burning sensation often associated with cystitis, providing relief and comfort to those suffering from the condition. The cream is easy to apply and can be used in combination with other treatments, such as antibiotics, to help manage the symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Echinacea purpurea herbal creams that help with cystitis, you'll need to start by drying the Echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in a warm, dry place. You can then chop them into small pieces and steep them in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, for several weeks to create a medicinal infused oil. After straining the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter, you can mix it with a thickening agent, like beeswax or shea butter, to create a creamy texture. Next, you'll need to heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each heating session, until the beeswax or shea butter has melted and the mixture is smooth. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean container, let it cool and harden, and your Echinacea purpurea herbal cream is ready to use. It's essential to note that before using any herbal cream, especially for medical conditions like cystitis, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional or a certified herbalist for proper guidance and dosage instructions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, is a plant-based ingredient that can be found in some creams. Its active compounds, particularly flavonoids and terpenes, have anti-inflammatory properties. This can be beneficial for people suffering from cystitis, an infection of the urinary tract that causes pain and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of Achillea millefolium can help soothe and calm the affected area, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties can help fight off the bacterial infection that causes cystitis, promoting the healing process. Some studies suggest that Achillea millefolium may also have a natural diuretic effect, helping to flush out the bacteria from the urinary tract. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for treating cystitis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Echinacea purpurea herbal cream that helps with cystitis, you will need some dried Echinacea purpurea roots, carrier oils like coconut or olive oil, and a base cream like aloe vera or shea butter. Start by making an infusion of the dried Echinacea purpurea roots in hot water. Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain it and let it cool down. Next, mix the cooled infusion with your chosen carrier oil in a 1:1 ratio. For example, if you have 100ml of infusion, add 100ml of coconut oil. Let this mixture sit for a few hours or overnight to allow the Echinacea to infuse into the oil. Once the infusion has been fully incorporated into the oil, you can mix it with your base cream in a 1:1 ratio again. For example, if you have 100ml of infused oil, add 100ml of aloe vera cream. Stir the mixture well and transfer it to a container. Let the cream sit for a few days to allow the Echinacea to mature and the ingredients to blend together. Once it's ready, you can use it to help soothe and calm the symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for cystitis

Ellagic acid is good for cystitis because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria that cause urinary tract infections.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon, also known as cranberry, has been used for years to help with urinary problems, including cystitis. This is because the active compounds in cranberry, such as proanthocyanidins, can help prevent bacteria from sticking to the bladder and urinary tract walls. When bacteria stick to these walls, they can cause infections, which can lead to cystitis symptoms like painful urination and frequent trips to the bathroom. By preventing bacteria from sticking, Vaccinium macrocarpon creams may help prevent these infections from occurring in the first place.  The bacteria that typically cause cystitis, like E. coli, are often found in the gut and can easily enter the urinary tract through the urethra. Once inside, they can multiply and cause an infection. Vaccinium macrocarpon creams may help prevent this from happening by creating an environment that is not favorable for bacterial growth. This can lead to a reduction in symptoms and the need for antibiotics, which can have unpleasant side effects. It's worth noting that while Vaccinium macrocarpon creams may be helpful in preventing cystitis, they may not be effective in treating an existing infection. In these cases, antibiotics are usually necessary to clear up the infection. However, as a preventative measure, Vaccinium macrocarpon creams may be a useful addition to a healthy lifestyle that includes staying hydrated and avoiding irritants like certain foods and fabrics.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Echinacea purpurea herbal cream that helps with cystitis, start by drying the flowers and roots of the Echinacea purpurea plant. This can be done by tying the stems together and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the Echinacea is completely dry, you can make a herbal infusion by steeping it in boiling water for 10-15 minutes. Let the mixture cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Next, mix the cooled Echinacea infusion with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a ratio of about 1 part Echinacea to 3 parts oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl, then whip it with an electric mixer until it thickens and becomes a creamy consistency. Finally, add a natural preservative, such as vitamin E oil or beeswax, to the cream to help extend its shelf life and prevent spoilage. Once the cream has cooled and set, it's ready to use as a topical treatment for cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium macrocarpon herbal creams for cystitis

Flavonoids are good for cystitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract, making it easier to urinate and alleviating symptoms of the condition.
Polyphenols are good for cystitis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the urinary tract, thereby relieving symptoms of the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams are sometimes used to help with cystitis. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the bladder and urinary tract. Cystitis is often caused by an infection, and Echinacea purpurea may have antibacterial properties that can help fight off the infection. Additionally, it has antioxidant properties that can help protect the bladder and urinary tract from damage caused by the infection. Some people find that using Echinacea purpurea creams helps to soothe and calm the bladder and urinary tract, making it easier to urinate and reducing the urge to urinate frequently.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Echinacea purpurea herbal cream that may help with cystitis, start by infusing dried Echinacea roots in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Fill a clean glass jar with about 1/4 cup of dried Echinacea roots and cover them with 1 cup of carrier oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it infuse for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, mix 1 tablespoon of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of a natural cream base, such as beeswax or shea butter, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax or shea butter has melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the cream from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a spreadable consistency. Pour the cream into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator to prevent spoilage. This Echinacea cream can be applied topically to the affected area to help soothe and calm the skin, potentially relieving cystitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for cystitis

intro_desc: Cystitis, an inflammation of the bladder, is a painful and uncomfortable condition that affects many people. Herbal creams can be a helpful remedy for this issue. Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, is a key herb in these creams. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and discomfort associated with cystitis. Urtica dioica also has antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off the bacteria that often cause cystitis.  Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, is another important herb in these creams. It has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the affected area. It can also help to reduce pain and discomfort.  Fixing the issue of cystitis is important because it can be a recurring problem that can affect a person's quality of life. If left untreated, it can lead to more serious complications, such as kidney damage or infection. By using herbal creams containing Urtica dioica and Hypericum perforatum, you can help to alleviate symptoms and prevent future episodes. This can improve your life by reducing the frequency and severity of cystitis attacks, allowing you to live a more normal and active life.
supplementary_best: To remedy cystitis, a combination of Equisetum arvense and Urtica dioica herbal creams is recommended. Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail, has anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties that help reduce swelling and increase urine production, which can help alleviate the symptoms of cystitis. Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the bladder and urinary tract. Together, these two herbs can help reduce inflammation, promote healing, and alleviate symptoms such as pain and frequent urination.  The combination is also enhanced by the addition of Hypericum perforatum, or St. John's Wort, which has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infections and promote healing. However, the best results come from combining these three herbs, rather than any other combination.
supplementary_avoid: If you have cystitis, you should be careful with certain herbal creams. Some creams contain herbs like Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) which can irritate the bladder and make symptoms worse. Goldenseal contains a chemical called berberine that can reduce urine flow and increase the risk of kidney damage. Another herb to avoid is Saw Palmetto (Serenoa repens), which can slow down urine flow and worsen symptoms. Yarrow (Achillea millefolium) is also not recommended as it can cause the urine to become cloudy and painful to urinate. Some creams may also contain Willow Bark (Salix alba), which contains salicin, a chemical similar to aspirin that can irritate the bladder and worsen symptoms. Furthermore, some herbal creams may contain Red Clover (Trifolium pratense), which can increase urine acidity and worsen symptoms. Finally, Creams containing Sarsaparilla (Smilax ornata) are also not recommended, as it can irritate the bladder and worsen symptoms. It's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal creams.
supplementary_other_remedies: Baking soda can help with cystitis by reducing acidity in the urine, which can help to alleviate burning and discomfort. To use baking soda, mix 1 teaspoon with water to create a solution, and drink it 2-3 times a day. Another natural remedy is cranberry juice, which helps to prevent bacteria from sticking to the bladder wall, reducing the risk of infection. Cranberry juice can be consumed as is or added to water for a less concentrated taste. Some people also find relief from cystitis by drinking plenty of water and increasing their intake of vitamin C-rich foods, such as citrus fruits and leafy greens. These foods help to boost the immune system, which can help to fight off infections and promote healing. Probiotics, found in foods like yogurt and kefir, can also help to maintain a healthy balance of good bacteria in the bladder and urinary tract, reducing the risk of infection.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with cystitis often experience other health problems that can be helped with medicinal herbs. One common issue is urinary tract infections (UTIs) that spread to the kidneys, causing a condition called pyelonephritis. This is because the bacteria that cause cystitis can travel up the ureters to the kidneys. The herb uva ursi has antimicrobial properties that help fight these bacteria. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling in the kidneys.  Another issue is incontinence, which is when people have trouble controlling their bladder. The herb corn silk helps with this by soothing the bladder and reducing spasms. It also helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause more infections. Some people with cystitis also experience painful sex, known as dyspareunia. The herb goldenseal has antimicrobial properties that help fight off bacteria that can cause infections during sex. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and swelling.  Some people with cystitis may also experience kidney stones, which are hard lumps that form in the kidneys when minerals and salts build up. The herb dandelion root helps to break down these stones by increasing urine production and reducing inflammation in the kidneys. This can help to pass the stones out of the body more easily.  Lastly, some people with cystitis may experience fatigue and low energy levels. The herb ashwagandha helps to boost the immune system and reduce stress levels, which can help to fight off infections and reduce fatigue. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce pain and swelling in the body.  It's worth noting that while these herbs can be helpful in managing symptoms of cystitis, they should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. People with cystitis should always consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
